title: CUSTOS API – Federal government costs
text: CUSTOS API (https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/docs/custos/) provides cost data Federal Government Cost Portal (Portal de Custos Governo Federal). breaks costs six categories: active staff, retired staff, pensioners, depreciation, transfers, costs. parameters API optional — can call function without arguments retrieve full dataset.

title: CUSTOS API – Federal government costs
text: CUSTOS API slow. server default 250 rows per page (package increases 1000), unfiltered queries can still return hundreds thousands rows across many pages, frequent HTTP 504 timeouts. Always filter queries least: year + month (reduce time range) org_level1 + org_level2 (reduce specific organization) legal_nature (reduce legal nature category) max_rows (set hard cap testing) package retries automatically 504 errors (5 times progressive backoff), narrower filters prevent first place.

title: CUSTOS API – Federal government costs
text: six functions share optional filters: SIORG codes automatically zero-padded: can pass 244, "244", "000244" — produce query.

title: CUSTOS API – Federal government costs
code: library(tesouror) library(dplyr)  # Step 1: Look up SIORG codes for the organization you want orgaos <- get_siorg_organizations(power_code = 1, sphere_code = 1) mec <- orgaos |> filter(sigla == "MEC")          # code 244 inep <- orgaos |> filter(sigla == "INEP")         # code 249  # Step 2: Query CUSTOS with org filters (much faster than unfiltered!) # Active staff costs for INEP, all months of 2023 ativos_inep <- get_costs_active_staff(   year = 2023,   org_level1 = 244,     # MEC — auto-padded to "000244"   org_level2 = 249      # INEP — auto-padded to "000249" )  # Pensioner costs for INEP, June 2023 only pensionistas_inep <- get_costs_pensioners(   year = 2023, month = 6,   org_level1 = 244,   org_level2 = 249 )  # Quick test: just grab the first 100 rows sample <- get_costs_active_staff(   year = 2023, month = 6,   legal_nature = 3,   max_rows = 100 )

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: tesouror package provides unified R interface Brazilian National Treasury (Tesouro Nacional) open data APIs. covers six major data sources: functions return tidy tibbles, use -memory caching, Portuguese-named (matching API) English-named versions.

title: Getting started with tesouror
code: # From CRAN (when available): install.packages("tesouror")  # Development version from GitHub: # remotes::install_github("StrategicProjects/tesouror")

title: Getting started with tesouror
code: library(tesouror)  # List all government entities entes <- get_entes()  # Get RREO data for Tocantins (IBGE code 17) rreo <- get_rreo(   an_exercicio = 2022, nr_periodo = 6,   co_tipo_demonstrativo = "RREO",   no_anexo = "RREO-Anexo 01",   co_esfera = "E", id_ente = 17 )  # Same query using English aliases rreo <- get_budget_report(   fiscal_year = 2022, period = 6,   report_type = "RREO",   appendix = "RREO-Anexo 01",   sphere = "E", entity_id = 17 )  # Federal government costs (always filter by org to avoid slow queries!) custos <- get_custos_pessoal_ativo(   ano = 2023, mes = 6,   organizacao_n1 = 244,  # MEC (auto-padded)   organizacao_n2 = 249   # INEP )  # Constitutional transfers (codes are Treasury-internal, NOT IBGE!) estados <- get_tc_estados() pe_code <- estados$codigo[estados$nome == "Pernambuco"] tc <- get_tc_por_estados(p_estado = pe_code, p_ano = 2023)  # Education spending data from SIOPE indicadores <- get_siope_indicators(year = 2023, period = 6, state = "PE")

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: functions cache responses -memory R session. means repeated calls parameters instantaneous. clear cache:
code: tesouror_clear_cache()

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: Every function two versions. Portuguese version uses exact API parameter names, English version uses descriptive English names: call endpoint return data. See vignette("siconfi"), vignette("custos"), vignette("sadipem"), vignette("siorg"), vignette("transferencias"), vignette("siope") articles API-specific details.
code: # Portuguese (API-native) get_dca(an_exercicio = 2022, id_ente = 17)  # English get_annual_accounts(fiscal_year = 2022, entity_id = 17)

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: Every function verbose parameter prints full API URL called. useful debugging testing browser/curl:
code: # Per call: get_costs_active_staff(year = 2023, month = 6, org_level1 = 244, verbose = TRUE) #> ℹ API call: https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/ords/custos/tt/pessoal_ativo?ano=2023&mes=6&organizacao_n1=000244&limit=1000  # Or globally for the session: options(tesouror.verbose = TRUE) get_entes()  # will print the URL options(tesouror.verbose = FALSE)  # turn off

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: ORDS-based APIs (SICONFI, CUSTOS, SADIPEM) return paginated results. page_size parameter controls many rows per page:
code: # CUSTOS defaults to 1000 rows/page (server default is only 250) custos <- get_costs_active_staff(   year = 2023, org_level1 = 244, org_level2 = 249 )  # Lower for quick tests: custos_sample <- get_costs_active_staff(   year = 2023, org_level1 = 244, org_level2 = 249,   page_size = 100, max_rows = 200 )  # SICONFI/SADIPEM default to server's 5000 rows/page (fast) entes <- get_entes()

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: API responses cleaned janitor::clean_names() ensure consistent snake_case column names (e.g., CO_IBGE becomes co_ibge).

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: Base URL: https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/ords/siconfi/tt/ Fiscal reports (RREO, RGF, DCA), accounting matrices (MSC), entity registry. Maintained STN. ORDS pagination (hasMore/offset) server default 5,000 rows/page. 18 functions (9 PT + 9 EN).

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: Base URL: https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/ords/custos/tt/ Cost data active/retired staff, pensioners, depreciation, transfers, costs. ORDS pagination default 1,000 rows/page (server default 250 slow; 5,000 causes timeouts). SIORG codes auto-padded (244 → "000244"). 12 functions (6 PT + 6 EN). Warning: Always filter organization level (organizacao_n1 + organizacao_n2) avoid downloading hundreds thousands rows.

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: Base URL: https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/ords/sadipem/tt/ PVL (public debt verification letters), credit operations, payment schedules, exchange rates, debt capacity. ORDS pagination server default 5,000 rows/page. 14 functions (7 PT + 7 EN).

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: Base URL: https://apiapex.tesouro.gov.br/aria/v1/transferencias_constitucionais/custom/ Constitutional transfers (FPE, FPM, FUNDEB, etc.). pagination (single response). Accepts vectors (c(1,2)) colon-separated strings ("1:2"). Uses Treasury-internal codes, IBGE. 14 functions (7 PT + 7 EN).

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: Base URL: https://estruturaorganizacional.dados.gov.br/ Federal organizational structure: ministries, autarchies, foundations. Used dictionary CUSTOS organization codes. pagination. 6 functions (3 PT + 3 EN).

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: Base URL: https://www.fnde.gov.br/olinda-ide/servico/DADOS_ABERTOS_SIOPE/versao/v1/odata/ Education spending data FNDE/MEC: revenues, expenses, indicators, staff compensation. OData pagination ($top/$skip) default 1,000 rows/page. Supports server-side filter (OData $filter), orderby, select. 16 functions (8 PT + 8 EN). Tip: Use filter = "NOM_MUNI eq 'Recife'" narrow results server. Column names filter/select/orderby must use original API names (uppercase). Run toupper(names(result)) max_rows = 1 query discover .

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: 80 functions share features: Retries: 5 attempts progressive backoff (3s, 6s, 9s, 12s, 15s) HTTP 500/502/503/504/429 connection failures. HTTP 400/404 retried. Caching: -memory per session. Clear tesouror_clear_cache(). verbose mode: Per call (verbose = TRUE) globally (options(tesouror.verbose = TRUE)). max_rows: Cap number rows returned (adjusts limit $top automatically). Column cleaning: janitor::clean_names() applied responses. Bilingual: Portuguese (API-native) English aliases every function. Error messages: Friendly, actionable messages URL hints. HTTP 400 errors suggest checking column names filter/select.

title: SADIPEM API – Public debt & credit operations
text: SADIPEM API (https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/docs/sadipem/) provides data Public Debt Verification Letters (PVL) — process subnational entities (states municipalities) request authorization credit operations debt management National Treasury.

title: SADIPEM API – Public debt & credit operations
text: usual workflow first search PVL requests, use id_pleito value results fetch detailed information. Important: id_pleito database ID (e.g., 40353), row number. Use pvl$id_pleito[1] get value first row, pvl$id_pleito[40353] (tries access row 40,353).
code: library(tesouror) library(dplyr)  # Step 1: Search PVLs for Pernambuco pvl_pe <- get_debt_requests(state = "PE")  # Step 2: Pick an id_pleito VALUE from the results id <- pvl_pe$id_pleito[1]  # Step 3: Get details using that id_pleito pagamentos <- get_credit_payment_schedule(request_id = id) cdp <- get_debt_capacity(request_id = id)

title: SADIPEM API – Public debt & credit operations
text: get_pvl_status() / get_pvl_tramitacao() function returns data approved (deferred) non-credit operation PVLs. Filter status == "Deferido" first:
code: pvl_deferidos <- pvl_pe |> filter(status == "Deferido") if (nrow(pvl_deferidos) > 0) {   status <- get_pvl_status(request_id = pvl_deferidos$id_pleito[1]) }

title: SICONFI API – Fiscal data
text: SICONFI API (https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/docs/siconfi/) provides fiscal data Brazilian states, municipalities, Federal District. includes budget execution reports (RREO), fiscal management reports (RGF), annual accounts declarations (DCA), accounting balances matrices (MSC).

title: SICONFI API – Fiscal data
code: library(tesouror) library(dplyr)  # All entities entes <- get_entes()  # Filter states only estados <- entes |> filter(esfera == "E")

title: SICONFI API – Fiscal data

title: SIOPE API – Education spending data
text: SIOPE (Sistema de Informações sobre Orçamentos Públicos em Educação) provides education spending data states municipalities. system maintained FNDE (Fundo Nacional de Desenvolvimento da Educação) API uses OData-style interface hosted https://www.fnde.gov.br/olinda-ide/servico/DADOS_ABERTOS_SIOPE/. Data covers revenues, expenses, indicators, official contacts, staff compensation related public education spending.

title: SIOPE API – Education spending data
text: functions share three required parameters: Exception: get_siope_remuneracao() / get_siope_compensation() 4th required parameter: mes / month (1-12).

what: Performance tip: use filter to reduce downloads
title: SIOPE API – Education spending data
text: SIOPE API returns municipalities given state. single call São Paulo (state = "SP") returns 645+ rows. Use filter parameter apply server-side filtering download data need. much faster downloading everything filtering locally dplyr::filter().

previous_headings: Performance tip: use filter to reduce downloads
title: SIOPE API – Education spending data
code: # SLOW: downloads all 185 municipalities, then filters locally dados_pe <- get_siope_general_data(year = 2023, period = 6, state = "PE") recife <- dplyr::filter(dados_pe, nom_muni == "Recife")  # FAST: server returns only Recife's data recife <- get_siope_general_data(   year = 2023, period = 6, state = "PE",   filter = "NOM_MUNI eq 'Recife'" )  # Filter by IBGE code recife <- get_siope_general_data(   year = 2023, period = 6, state = "PE",   filter = "COD_MUNI eq 261160" )

title: SIORG – Organizational Structure
text: SIORG API provides official organizational structure Brazilian Federal Executive Branch. SIORG codes identify ministries, agencies, departments, organizational units. matters tesouror: CUSTOS API uses SIORG codes organizacao_n1, organizacao_n2, organizacao_n3 parameters. SIORG functions serve dictionaries look codes.

title: SIORG – Organizational Structure
code: library(tesouror) library(dplyr)  # List all federal executive branch organizations orgaos <- get_siorg_organizations(power_code = 1, sphere_code = 1)  # Key columns: #   codigo_unidade     – SIORG code (use as organizacao_n1 in CUSTOS) #   codigo_unidade_pai – parent unit code #   nome               – organization name #   sigla              – abbreviation #   codigo_tipo_unidade – "orgao" or "entidade" #   codigo_natureza_juridica – 1=Empresa Pública, 2=Fundação, 3=Adm.Direta, 4=Autarquia  # Find the Ministry of Education mec <- orgaos |> filter(grepl("Educação", nome), codigo_tipo_unidade == "orgao") mec |> select(codigo_unidade, nome, sigla) # codigo_unidade = "244" → this is the org_level1 for CUSTOS

title: SIORG – Organizational Structure
text: codigo_unidade_pai column lets navigate hierarchy. Units whose codigo_unidade_pai matches ministry’s code direct children (N2 level). children N3.
code: # All organizations under MEC (direct children = N2) mec_children <- orgaos |>   filter(codigo_unidade_pai == "244") |>  # MEC's code   select(codigo_unidade, nome, sigla, codigo_tipo_unidade) mec_children # Examples: INEP (249), FNDE (253), CAPES (478), etc.  # For deeper structure, use get_siorg_structure() estrutura <- get_siorg_structure(unit_code = 244) estrutura |> select(codigo_unidade, codigo_unidade_pai, nome, sigla)

title: SIORG – Organizational Structure
text: SIORG returns plain codes like "244", CUSTOS API expects zero-padded 6-digit strings like "000244". package pads automatically — just pass SIORG code directly:
code: # Active staff costs for MEC — "244" is auto-padded to "000244" custos_mec <- get_costs_active_staff(   year = 2023,   org_level1 = 244  # numeric or character, both work )  # Drill down to INEP custos_inep <- get_costs_active_staff(   year = 2023,   org_level1 = 244,   org_level2 = 249  # auto-padded to "000249" )

what: Step 4: Get details of a single unit
title: SIORG – Organizational Structure
code: # Full details for AGU (code 46) agu <- get_siorg_unit(unit_code = 46) agu |> select(codigo_unidade, nome, sigla, codigo_natureza_juridica)

title: SIORG – Organizational Structure
text: CUSTOS API uses SIORG codes 6-digit zero-padded strings (e.g., "000244"). package auto-pads plain codes, can pass 244, "244", "000244" — work. CUSTOS response also includes co_organizacao_n0 (top-level authority, e.g., Presidência da República = "000026"), query parameter appears results.

title: SIORG – Organizational Structure
text: Key columns returned get_siorg_orgaos() / get_siorg_organizations():

title: Transferências Constitucionais API
text: Transferências Constitucionais API provides data constitutional transfers Federal Government states municipalities. transfers include FPE (Fundo de Participação dos Estados), FPM (Fundo de Participação dos Municípios), FUNDEB, mandatory transfers. Base URL: https://apiapex.tesouro.gov.br/aria/v1/transferencias_constitucionais/custom

title: Transferências Constitucionais API
text: functions use get_tc_ prefix. filter parameters use numeric codes, names abbreviations. Use lookup functions (get_tc_transferencias, get_tc_estados, get_tc_municipios) obtain codes. Multiple values separated colons (:) — e.g., p_estado = "1:2" Acre Alagoas.

title: Transferências Constitucionais API
text: filter parameters use numeric codes internal API (IBGE codes). Always look codes first.
code: library(tesouror)  # Transfer types — returns codigo + nome tipos <- get_tc_transfer_types() tipos # e.g., codigo=10 nome="FUNDEB", codigo=14 nome="AJUSTE FUNDEB"  # State codes — returns codigo + nome estados <- get_tc_states() estados # e.g., codigo=16 nome="Pernambuco" (NOT the IBGE code!)  # Municipality codes — filter by the state code from above pe_code <- estados$codigo[estados$nome == "Pernambuco"] mun_pe <- get_tc_municipalities(state_code = pe_code) mun_pe

title: Transferências Constitucionais API
code: # Use the state code obtained from get_tc_states() pe_code <- estados$codigo[estados$nome == "Pernambuco"]  # All transfers for Pernambuco in 2023 tc_pe <- get_tc_by_state(state_code = pe_code, year = 2023)  # FUNDEB + AJUSTE FUNDEB — pass a vector of transfer type codes tc_fundeb <- get_tc_by_state(   state_code = pe_code,   year = 2023,   transfer_type = c(10, 14) )  # Multiple states, Jan-Mar 2023 — vectors work everywhere ac_code <- estados$codigo[estados$nome == "Acre"] al_code <- estados$codigo[estados$nome == "Alagoas"] tc_multi <- get_tc_by_state(   state_code = c(ac_code, al_code),   year = 2023,   month = 1:3 )  # Same query with detailed breakdown tc_det <- get_tc_by_state_detail(   state_code = c(ac_code, al_code),   year = 2023,   month = 1:3 )

title: Transferências Constitucionais API
code: # Look up municipality codes pe_code <- estados$codigo[estados$nome == "Pernambuco"] mun_pe <- get_tc_municipalities(state_code = pe_code) recife_code <- mun_pe$codigo[mun_pe$nome == "Recife"]  # All municipalities in Pernambuco in 2023 tc_mun <- get_tc_by_municipality(state_code = pe_code, year = 2023)  # Specific municipality: Recife tc_recife <- get_tc_by_municipality_detail(   state_code = pe_code,   municipality = recife_code,   year = 2023 )

title: Transferências Constitucionais API
text: multi-value parameters accept either colon-separated string ("1:2:3") R vector (c(1, 2, 3)).

what: tesouror: Access Brazilian National Treasury Open Data APIs — tesouror-package
title: tesouror: Access Brazilian National Treasury Open Data APIs — tesouror-package
text: Provides unified interface access open data Brazilian National Treasury (Tesouro Nacional) related government APIs. Covers six data sources: SICONFI https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/docs/siconfi/ fiscal reports (RREO, RGF, DCA, MSC) entity information; CUSTOS https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/docs/custos/ federal government cost data; SADIPEM https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/docs/sadipem/ public debt credit operations; Transferencias Constitucionais https://apiapex.tesouro.gov.br/aria/v1/transferencias_constitucionais/docs constitutional transfers states municipalities; SIORG https://estruturaorganizacional.dados.gov.br federal organizational structure; SIOPE https://www.fnde.gov.br/olinda-ide/servico/DADOS_ABERTOS_SIOPE/versao/v1/aplicacao education spending data FNDE/MEC. Features automatic pagination, -memory caching, retry logic, tidy output.

text: tesouror expands former siconfir package unified interface Brazilian National Treasury open data APIs.

title: tesouror 0.1.0
text: SICONFI — Fiscal reports (RREO, RGF, DCA, MSC) entity data (functions original siconfir package). CUSTOS — Federal government cost data (active/retired staff, pensioners, depreciation, transfers, costs). SADIPEM — Public debt verification letters (PVL), credit operations, payment schedules, debt capacity. Transferências Constitucionais — Constitutional transfers states municipalities (FPE, FPM, FUNDEB, etc.). SIORG — Federal organizational structure (organs, entities, departments). SIORG codes serve dictionary values organizacao_n1/n2/n3 parameters CUSTOS API. SIOPE — Education spending data FNDE/MEC (general data, revenues, expenses, indicators, compensation). Uses FNDE’s OData-style API.

title: tesouror 0.1.0
text: Bilingual interface: every function Portuguese (API-native) English-named versions English parameter names. -memory caching across APIs (tesouror_clear_cache()). Automatic pagination ORDS-style responses (SICONFI, CUSTOS, SADIPEM) OData-style responses (SIOPE), per-page progress reporting. page_size parameter control rows per page (CUSTOS defaults 1000 instead server’s slow default 250; SIOPE defaults 1000). max_rows parameter paginated functions cap number rows returned. Automatically adjusts limit/$top avoid fetching unnecessary data. Retry logic: 5 attempts progressive backoff (3s, 6s, 9s, 12s, 15s) HTTP 500/502/503/504/429 connection failures. HTTP 400/404 retried produce actionable error messages. verbose parameter every function print full API URL debugging testing browser/curl. Can set globally options(tesouror.verbose = TRUE). janitor::clean_names() applied responses consistent snake_case column names. CUSTOS: SIORG codes auto-padded (244 → "000244"). SIOPE: OData $filter, $orderby, $select support server-side filtering, sorting, column selection. Transferências: multi-value parameters accept R vectors (c(1, 2, 3)) addition colon-separated strings ("1:2:3"). Friendly error messages retry logic transient failures. HTTP 400 errors parse server messages suggest checking column names. NA validation required parameters. Tidy tibble output whitespace trimming.
